subject: UX & Research
title: The Windows 8 Start Screen
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Windows 8 Start Screen.

hook.text: Microsoft removed the Start button in Windows 8. Millions of desktop users couldn't find their programs.

buildup.text: The new Start Screen used full-screen tiles designed for tablets. Desktop users had no touch screens.

discovery.text: Users couldn't find the shutdown button. It was hidden behind a swipe gesture on a device without touch.

twist.text: Windows 8 adoption was the lowest since Vista. Microsoft brought the Start button back in Windows 8.1.

climax.text: Forcing a touch paradigm on mouse users violated their mental model. The familiar was replaced with alien.

punchline.text: Don't replace what works with what's trendy.

quiz.question: Why did Windows 8's Start Screen fail on desktops?
options: It forced a touch-first paradigm on mouse and keyboard users
